steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage establishes two facts: (1) ferritic stainless steel, used for cooking utensils, has a high iron concentration that gives it magnetic properties; (2) austenitic stainless steel, used for mining and chemical equipment, has a nickel-derived crystalline structure and lacks that iron-based magnetism. The blank must complete a logical conclusion from these facts.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — cooking utensils will have greater magnetic properties than stainless steel used to manufacture mining and chemical equipment will.: Ferritic (cooking utensils) has iron-based magnetism; austenitic (mining/chemical) does not. This follows directly from the passage. ✓
  • Choice B — mining and chemical equipment will have a concentration of chromium lower than 10.5%, while stainless steel used for washing machines will not.: The passage says ferritic has at least 10.5% chromium and austenitic has less, but makes no specific claim about washing machines. This introduces unsupported details. ✗
  • Choice C — cooking utensils will have a face-centered cubic crystalline structure.: The face-centered cubic structure is a property of austenitic steel, not ferritic. Cooking utensils use ferritic steel. ✗
  • Choice D — compensators will likely have a similar crystalline structure to stainless steel used to manufacture cooking utensils.: Compensators are not mentioned in the passage; this cannot be inferred. ✗
That is the correct answer.
